summary:
A traumatic neuroma develops after mandibular fractures when nerves are injured or compressed during displacement; this condition presents as localized tenderness and a moveable mass at the site of previous trauma—consistent with findings here. This makes it the most likely cause based on both mechanism and presentation compared to other possible diagnoses such as cysts or tumors which do not typically present with these specific features related directly to prior mechanical damage.

Note: Do NOT include any commentary about correctness or confidence levels beyond what’s stated explicitly within the provided text.
